STAT 1223 Project Statistical Analysis

Packages

library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.5
## ✔ forcats   1.0.0     ✔ stringr   1.5.1
## ✔ ggplot2   3.5.0     ✔ tibble    3.2.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.3     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
## ✔ purrr     1.0.2     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(zoo)
## Warning: package 'zoo' was built under R version 4.3.3
## 
## Attaching package: 'zoo'
## 
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     as.Date, as.Date.numeric
library(lmtest)
## Warning: package 'lmtest' was built under R version 4.3.3

Load Dataset

Treasury <- read.csv("Merged_Data.csv", sep=",", header = TRUE)

Clean Data

  # Convert date
Treasury$date <- as.Date(Treasury$Date)

str(Treasury)
## 'data.frame':    120 obs. of  10 variables:
##  $ Date          : chr  "2015-01-31" "2015-02-28" "2015-03-31" "2015-04-30" ...
##  $ US_10Y        : num  1.68 2 1.94 2.05 2.12 2.35 2.2 2.21 2.06 2.16 ...
##  $ CN10Y         : num  2.01 2.12 2.15 2.11 2.16 ...
##  $ Inflation_Rate: num  0.254 0.269 0.104 0.33 0.277 ...
##  $ FEDFUNDS_RATE : num  0.11 0.11 0.12 0.12 0.13 0.13 0.14 0.14 0.12 0.12 ...
##  $ GOLD          : num  1278 1213 1183 1182 1189 ...
##  $ SP500         : num  1995 2104 2068 2086 2107 ...
##  $ UNRATE        : num  5.5 5.4 5.4 5.6 5.3 5.2 5.1 5 5 5.1 ...
##  $ USD           : num  94.8 95.2 98.4 94.6 96.9 ...
##  $ date          : Date, format: "2015-01-31" "2015-02-28" ...
  # Create original dataset without unnecessary Date columns, ready to use to model
Treasury_original <- Treasury %>% select(-Date, -date)

  # COVID dummy
Treasury_original$covid <- ifelse(as.Date(Treasury$date) >= as.Date("2020-03-01") &
  as.Date(Treasury$date) <= as.Date("2021-06-01"), 1, 0)

  # SP500 change (first-difference)
Treasury_original$SP500_diff <- c(NA, diff(Treasury$SP500))

 # Lagged variable (inflation)
Treasury_original$inflation_lag2 <- dplyr::lag(Treasury_original$Inflation_Rate, 2)

Drop NA

treasury_na <- Treasury_original %>% drop_na(inflation_lag2, SP500_diff)

PLOTS: Check for linearity

library(ggplot2)
Treasury_long <- Treasury %>% select(-Date) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(US_10Y, date), names_to = "Predictor", values_to = "Value")
predictor_names <- unique(Treasury_long$Predictor)
for (var in predictor_names) {
  Treasury_plot_data <- Treasury_long %>%
    filter(Predictor == var)
   scale_factor <- max(Treasury_plot_data$Value, na.rm = TRUE) / max(Treasury$US_10Y, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  p <- ggplot(Treasury_plot_data, aes(x = date, y = Value)) +
    geom_line(aes(y = Value), color = "red", size = 1) +
    
    # Right y-axis: US_10Y time series (rescaled)
    geom_line(aes(y = US_10Y * scale_factor), color = "blue", size = 1) +
    labs(title = paste("Time-Based Relationship: US_10Y vs", var),
         x = "Date",
         y = var) +
      
    scale_y_continuous(
      sec.axis = sec_axis(~ . / scale_factor, name = "US_10Y")  # Convert back to original US_10Y scale
    ) +
    
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
          axis.text = element_text(size = 12))

    print(p)  # Print each plot separately
}
## Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

for (var in predictor_names) {
  
  p <- ggplot(Treasury_long %>% filter(Predictor == var), aes(x = Value, y = US_10Y)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +  # Scatter points
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red", se = TRUE) +  # Linear regression line
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "blue", se = FALSE) +  # LOESS curve (for comparison)
    labs(title = paste("Linearity Check: US_10Y vs", var),
         x = var,
         y = "US_10Y") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
          axis.text = element_text(size = 12))

  print(p)
}
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

Collinearity check

# Correlation Matrix
cor(Treasury_original %>% select(US_10Y, FEDFUNDS_RATE,GOLD, Inflation_Rate, SP500, UNRATE, USD))
##                    US_10Y FEDFUNDS_RATE         GOLD Inflation_Rate      SP500
## US_10Y          1.0000000     0.8960308  0.425835784    -0.02890540  0.5133422
## FEDFUNDS_RATE   0.8960308     1.0000000  0.590126807    -0.07238450  0.6271218
## GOLD            0.4258358     0.5901268  1.000000000     0.29438407  0.9447740
## Inflation_Rate -0.0289054    -0.0723845  0.294384070     1.00000000  0.3755962
## SP500           0.5133422     0.6271218  0.944773990     0.37559618  1.0000000
## UNRATE         -0.5703037    -0.4437917 -0.009429708    -0.17877910 -0.1929470
## USD             0.7062275     0.6953242  0.450178729    -0.09543795  0.4522635
##                      UNRATE         USD
## US_10Y         -0.570303749  0.70622748
## FEDFUNDS_RATE  -0.443791661  0.69532418
## GOLD           -0.009429708  0.45017873
## Inflation_Rate -0.178779099 -0.09543795
## SP500          -0.192946960  0.45226347
## UNRATE          1.000000000 -0.25670154
## USD            -0.256701536  1.00000000

Collinearity check after dropping problematic variables

cor_data <- treasury_na %>% select(US_10Y, FEDFUNDS_RATE, GOLD, SP500_diff, USD, UNRATE, inflation_lag2)

cor(cor_data)
##                     US_10Y FEDFUNDS_RATE         GOLD  SP500_diff         USD
## US_10Y          1.00000000    0.89603623  0.421242679  0.01005762  0.70438962
## FEDFUNDS_RATE   0.89603623    1.00000000  0.584324634  0.12395313  0.69278127
## GOLD            0.42124268    0.58432463  1.000000000  0.15727681  0.44514235
## SP500_diff      0.01005762    0.12395313  0.157276814  1.00000000 -0.08730819
## USD             0.70438962    0.69278127  0.445142349 -0.08730819  1.00000000
## UNRATE         -0.56847876   -0.44067804 -0.002523503  0.03756311 -0.25312352
## inflation_lag2  0.04011885   -0.03662009  0.236733824 -0.09404409  0.01482135
##                      UNRATE inflation_lag2
## US_10Y         -0.568478760     0.04011885
## FEDFUNDS_RATE  -0.440678043    -0.03662009
## GOLD           -0.002523503     0.23673382
## SP500_diff      0.037563112    -0.09404409
## USD            -0.253123518     0.01482135
## UNRATE          1.000000000    -0.30347750
## inflation_lag2 -0.303477497     1.00000000

Run initial regression

model <- lm(US_10Y ~ FEDFUNDS_RATE + USD + GOLD + UNRATE + SP500_diff + inflation_lag2 + covid, data = treasury_na)
summary(model)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = US_10Y ~ FEDFUNDS_RATE + USD + GOLD + UNRATE + SP500_diff + 
##     inflation_lag2 + covid, data = treasury_na)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -1.24385 -0.21743  0.01903  0.30079  0.75902 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                  Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)    -0.6921979  1.0472612  -0.661   0.5100    
## FEDFUNDS_RATE   0.4374028  0.0395212  11.068   <2e-16 ***
## USD             0.0301839  0.0115977   2.603   0.0105 *  
## GOLD           -0.0001835  0.0001558  -1.178   0.2413    
## UNRATE         -0.0608610  0.0404076  -1.506   0.1349    
## SP500_diff     -0.0002787  0.0002514  -1.109   0.2699    
## inflation_lag2  0.1505038  0.1685415   0.893   0.3738    
## covid          -0.3624336  0.2065947  -1.754   0.0822 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.4036 on 110 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.8688, Adjusted R-squared:  0.8604 
## F-statistic:   104 on 7 and 110 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16

VIF Check

require(regclass)
## Loading required package: regclass
## Loading required package: bestglm
## Loading required package: leaps
## Loading required package: VGAM
## Loading required package: stats4
## Loading required package: splines
## 
## Attaching package: 'VGAM'
## The following object is masked from 'package:lmtest':
## 
##     lrtest
## Loading required package: rpart
## Loading required package: randomForest
## randomForest 4.7-1.1
## Type rfNews() to see new features/changes/bug fixes.
## 
## Attaching package: 'randomForest'
## The following object is masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     combine
## The following object is masked from 'package:ggplot2':
## 
##     margin
## Important regclass change from 1.3:
## All functions that had a . in the name now have an _
## all.correlations -> all_correlations, cor.demo -> cor_demo, etc.
VIF(model)
##  FEDFUNDS_RATE            USD           GOLD         UNRATE     SP500_diff 
##       3.981718       2.349580       2.757539       3.562379       1.118802 
## inflation_lag2          covid 
##       1.572173       3.430981

Durbin-Watson Test for autocorrelation

dwtest(model)
## 
##  Durbin-Watson test
## 
## data:  model
## DW = 0.36253, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true autocorrelation is greater than 0
